title: benzoin hypoleucum
intro: Benzoin hypoleucum, also known as styrax, is a type of tree resin obtained from the Styrax leucopyrus or Styrax tonkinensis tree, native to Southeast Asia. The resin has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of benzoin hypoleucum include reducing pain and inflammation, promoting wound healing, and relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Benzoin hypoleucum can be prepared in various herbal forms, including essential oils, tinctures, and resins, which can be used topically or inhaled to harness its therapeutic benefits.
